Despite the existence of modern best-practice frameworks promoting early intervention and comprehensive care coordination, the retrieved evidence lacks concrete data demonstrating that older patients actually receive adequate stroke care. Without empirical validation of equitable access and successful outcomes, concluding "yes" or "maybe" is unsupported; therefore, the most medically grounded response based solely on available evidence is that older patients do not currently receive adequate stroke care.
